free_text: October supplement. Table .
proc. Int. Conf. on
Statistical Properties of Nuclei. S-wave analysis.
Experimental method.
-Average total neutron cross sections of heavy
elements at 2.7 keV-.
FRM reactor.
Monochromatic. 2.7 keV, energy spread 500 eV,
from resonance scattering from Na. (NIM,100,83,1972).
.Metal except where otherwise stated
.Strength function evaluated from total x-section.
.Table of data in text, EANDC(E)-150,40,1972.
strength-functions compiled in separate
subworks.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
corrected according to last EXFOR rules and Dict.
STATUS moved to Subent 001 from all others.
EN-RSL added to DATA in Subents 002-032.
EN corrected on EN-MIN and EN-MAX in Subents 033-058.
Ref. on Conf. corrected: 71ALBANY,327,1972 =>
71ALBANY,,327,1971 according to EXFOR rules.
EN moved to Subents.

.Estimated number of resonances in the 500 eV
energy spread is 25.
S-wave strength function obtained by subtraction
of p-wave strength and potential scattering. Details
in  71ALBANY,327,1971.
The errors quoted for S-wave strength take
into account uncertainties due to:
1. the measured cross sections,
2. corrections for potential scattering and p-waves,
3. the limited number of levels considered (this is
the dominant contribution to the resulting error)
SD: EN=2.7keV and EN-RSL=0.5 keV changed
on EN-MIN, EN-MAX according to EXFOR rules.
